2606.02049.title: Explainable Data-driven Deep Reinforcement Learning Methods for Optimal Energy Management in Buildings
2606.02049.paper_summary: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources into power systems, particularly in buildings equipped with photovoltaic (PV) panels and energy storage systems, introduces significant complexity in energy systems. Volatile power generation, varying electricity tariffs, and increased entities, e.g., PV systems, and heat pumps, have increased the complexity and made the system harder to operate. This leads to the demand for additional control and optimization routes including data-based controls, such as reinforcement learning. While deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has emerged as a promising solution to optimize building operations in dynamic and ever more complex environments, its black-box nature impedes user trust and practical adoption. This paper presents a framework for explainable deep reinforcement learning (XRL) applied to energy management in residential buildings. We demonstrate its usage on both synthetic data but also on real-world data from the Living Lab Energy Campus (LLEC) at KIT. We train and compare both on-policy and off-policy DRL agents on an expanded state space that incorporates real-time measurements (demand, PV generation, battery power, state of charge), external signals (dynamic electricity price, local weather data), calendrical and holiday indicators, and forecasts for demand and price. Our experimental results indicate that on-policy algorithms, particularly Advantage Actor Critic (A2C) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), outperform off-policy methods in terms of cumulative rewards and policy stability. To explain these models, we employ post-hoc interpretation techniques to elaborate the learned control policies. Our findings demonstrate that the XRL framework not only reduces electricity costs through optimal battery management, but also provides transparent, actionable insights into the agent's decision-making process.
2606.02049.paper_summary_zh: 可再生能源來源逐漸融入電力系統，特別是在配備光伏（PV）面板和儲能系統的建築中，為能源系統帶來了顯著的複雜性。波動的電力生成、變化的電價以及增加的實體，例如PV系統和熱泵，增加了系統的複雜性，使其更難以操作。這導致了對額外控制和優化路徑的需求，包括基於數據的控制，例如強化學習。雖然深度強化學習（DRL）已成為優化動態且日益複雜環境中建築運行的有希望的解決方案，但其黑箱特性阻礙了用戶的信任和實際採用。本文提出了一個可解釋的深度強化學習（XRL）框架，應用於住宅建築的能源管理。我們展示了其在合成數據和來自KIT的Living Lab Energy Campus（LLEC）的真實數據上的使用。我們在擴展的狀態空間上訓練並比較了基於策略和非基於策略的DRL代理，該狀態空間包含實時測量（需求、PV發電、電池功率、充電狀態）、外部信號（動態電價、當地氣象數據）、日曆和假日指標，以及需求和價格的預測。我們的實驗結果表明，基於策略的算法，特別是優勢演員評論家（A2C）和近端政策優化（PPO），在累積獎勵和政策穩定性方面優於非基於策略的方法。為了解釋這些模型，我們採用了事後解釋技術來闡述學習到的控制政策。我們的研究結果表明，XRL框架不僅通過最佳電池管理降低了電費，還提供了透明且可行的洞察，幫助理解代理的決策過程。
2606.02049.author: Hallah Shahid Butt et.al.
2606.02049.authors: Hallah Shahid Butt, Qiong Huang, Gökhan Demirel, Kevin Förderer, Erfan Tajalli-Ardekani, Simnon Waczowicz, Luigi Spatafora, Veit Hagenmeyer, Benjamin Schäfer
